verification_result.notes: All 12 tests pass (10 integration tests in tests/config_test.rs + 2 doctests). The 6 L3 #48 spec-named tests are all present verbatim in tests/config_test.rs: load_from_env_with_prefix_filters_unrelated_vars, load_from_env_defaults_port_8080, load_from_file_valid_json, load_from_file_missing_file_returns_io_error, builder_sets_defaults, missing_required_field_returns_missing_field_error. 4 additional tests round out the contract: load_from_file_missing_required_field_returns_missing_field_error, load_from_env_invalid_port_returns_parse_error, load_from_file_malformed_json_returns_parse_error, config_error_display_is_informative. Clippy clean (no warnings) under -D warnings. The crate is intentionally STANDALONE (empty [workspace] table in its own Cargo.toml) following the L3 #46 (pheno-errors) pattern, so the new crate does not pull the 56+ focus-*/connector-* crates in the root workspace into the build. The 3-variant ConfigError (MissingField, ParseError, IoError) is exactly the L3 DAG spec; a 4th variant would break the contract. Tests live in tests/config_test.rs (integration tests against the public API), not as inline #[cfg(test)] mod tests in src/lib.rs, per the L3 #48 spec's 'Tests in tests/config_test.rs' directive.

notes: V3 DAG L3 #48 — author canonical pheno-config Rust crate consolidating config loading (env vars + JSON file + programmatic builder) into a single typed Config struct consumed by every pheno-* service.

Crate is intentionally STANDALONE (empty [workspace] in its own Cargo.toml), not a member of the root Cargo.toml's [workspace.members] — mirrors L3 #46 (pheno-errors). This avoids touching the in-progress root Cargo.toml that other L3 agents are concurrently modifying, and keeps the new crate's test/build loop independent of the 56-crate root workspace.

Public API: pheno_config::load_from_env(prefix) reads <PREFIX>_URL (required), <PREFIX>_PORT (optional u16, default 8080), <PREFIX>_LOG_LEVEL (optional, default "info"), <PREFIX>_DB_PATH (required), <PREFIX>_FEATURE_FLAGS (optional comma-separated, default empty). Unrelated env vars (no prefix match) are filtered out (verified by load_from_env_with_prefix_filters_unrelated_vars).

pheno_config::load_from_file(path) reads a JSON file via serde_json. I/O errors propagate via the #[from] std::io::Error impl (IoError variant). serde_json missing-field errors are best-effort parsed to extract the field name and surfaced as MissingField; other serde errors are ParseError.

pheno_config::ConfigBuilder::new() yields sensible defaults: port=8080, log_level="info", feature_flags=Vec::new(). url and db_path are None and required. The builder has a Default impl (delegates to new()) for ergonomics with ..Default::default() spread patterns.

ConfigError is a closed 3-variant enum (thiserror derive): MissingField(String), ParseError{field, message}, IoError(#[from] std::io::Error). MissingField is the missing-required-field error. ParseError wraps any value-parse failure (bad u16 for PORT, malformed JSON, wrong JSON type). IoError wraps file I/O. No blanket From<E: Error> impl to avoid Rust coherence conflicts with the concrete io::Error impl.

Env-var test isolation: each test uses a unique prefix (PHENO_CONFIG_TEST_FULL, PHENO_CONFIG_TEST_DEFAULTS, PHENO_CONFIG_TEST_FILTER, PHENO_CONFIG_TEST_MISSING) and an RAII EnvGuard that restores prior env values on drop. No env-bleed between parallel tests. The feature_flags env-var is comma-split with whitespace trim and empty-filter, supporting 'a, b , c' style values.
